mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows persistent growth above 2% annually. This real rate compression signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k new jobs per month, signaling weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or Rank IC between PAYEMS and forward returns remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 550k annualized units. This dual demand contraction signals weakening consumer and residential investment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances despite dual demand signals, or joint RSAFS-HOUST interaction Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates to growth rates below 0.5% month-over-month, indicating slowing manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness. This signals reduced corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 62% of triggered instances despite supply weakness, or Rank IC between INDPRO and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) shows flat or negative growth. This labor market deterioration signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite labor market weakness, or joint UNRATE-PAYEMS Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to annualized rates below 2.0%, indicating weakening aggregate demand. This sub-trend growth signals earnings compression and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
regime_hint: sub-trend demand growth in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following sub-2% GDP growth, or Rank IC between GDPC1 and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation-adjusted real rates stay deeply negative. This persistent monetary accommodation supports equity valuations through reduced discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a baseline trend.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of all trading days during zero-rate regime, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating both supply-side weakness and labor market softening. This dual deterioration signals stagflationary pressure and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with supply and labor weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite supply-labor deterioration, or joint INDPRO-UNRATE Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
